# Multi-type Point Process in a nutshell

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="39%"}
![](images/slide_not_count/Bu-figure-1a.png){width="100%"}

:::
::: {.column width="59%"}
::: {style="font-size:1rem;"}

::: {.fragment}
- Notation:

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| $k=-1$ | transmission from $j$ to $i$ |
| $k=0$ | no transmission |
| $k=1$ | transmission from $i$ to $j$ |
| $c_{ij} \in \{-1,0,1\}$ | unknown type of $(i,j)$ |
| $x_{ij} \in [0,1] \times [0,1]$ | 2D mark of $(i,j)$ |
| $s_{ij} \in S \times S$ | coordinates of $(i,j)$ (their ages) |
:::

<div style="margin-top:3em;"></div>

::: {.fragment}
- Log likelihood if the type is known, ie $c_{ij} = k$:

<div style="font-size:1.5rem;">
$$
\begin{align*}
  s_{ij} &\sim \text{PP}\big( \gamma \: f(s_{ij}) \big) \\
  f(s_{ij}) &= \sum_k \: p_k \:  f_k(s_{ij})\\
  \text{logit} \: x_{ij} &\sim \text{2D-Normal-cdf}\big( \mu_k, \sigma_k^2 \big) 
\end{align*}
$$
</div>
:::

<div style="margin-top:3em;"></div>

::: {.fragment}
- Since type is unknown, use data augmentation for inference.
:::

:::
:::
:::
